frameworks.name: attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: discrete spatiotemporal patterns function as attractors in cortical network bursts

frameworks.name: Hebbian plasticity
frameworks.one_line: pathway-specific strengthening explains reshaping of attractor accessibility

core_question: How do attractors in cortical networks emerge, persist, or disappear in response to targeted stimulation?
key_assumptions: spontaneous network bursts can be classified into a discrete vocabulary of spatiotemporal patterns
these recurring patterns behave as discrete attractors of the network
Hebbian-like plasticity governs pathway weights into attractors
manipulations: electrical stimulation targeted at specific attractor patterns
comparison of spontaneous vs evoked activity
key_findings: spontaneous bursts in cultured cortical networks form a vocabulary of discrete attractor patterns
stimulating a specific attractor eliminates it from spontaneous activity
the same attractor remains robustly evoked by direct electrical stimulation
interpretations: stimulation strengthens specific input pathways into attractors while weakening non-evoked pathways
this Hebbian-like rewiring mechanistically explains the dissociation between spontaneous and evoked accessibility
